blocks.body: Future Continuous: Time Expressions — this time tomorrow, at 8 p.m. tomorrow, next week at this time, when, while, and similar markers.

{"level":"B1","intro":"Expressions such as this time tomorrow, at 8 p.m. tomorrow, tomorrow at ..., next week at this time, all day tomorrow, later tonight, when, while often go with Future Continuous.","rules":[{"label":"Exact future moment","color":"emerald","text":"These expressions show a point in the future when the action will be in progress.","example":"This time tomorrow, I will be flying to Rome."},{"label":"Longer future period","color":"blue","text":"Some markers highlight a longer future process.","example":"All day tomorrow, we will be working."},{"label":"When / While","color":"amber","text":"These words often connect Future Continuous with another action or future background.","example":"I will be cooking when you arrive. / While you are sleeping, we will be driving."}]}

{"title":"1. Useful time expressions","intro":"Start with the most common time markers for this tense.","items":[{"label":"Future point","title":"this time tomorrow / next week at this time","subtitle":"This time tomorrow, she will be taking the train."},{"label":"Clock time","title":"at 8 p.m. tomorrow / tomorrow at 6","subtitle":"Tomorrow at 6, I will be meeting the team."},{"label":"Longer period","title":"all day tomorrow / later tonight","subtitle":"Later tonight, they will be driving home."},{"label":"Linkers","title":"when / while","subtitle":"I will be working when you call. / While you are sleeping, we will be travelling."}]}

{"title":"2. How these expressions work","sections":[{"label":"Exact future moment","color":"emerald","description":"This time tomorrow, at 8 p.m. tomorrow, or tomorrow at ... show that the action will still be happening then.","examples":[{"en":"At 8 p.m. tomorrow, we will be having dinner with friends.","ua":"At that exact time, the action will be in progress."},{"en":"Tomorrow at 11, she will be giving a presentation.","ua":"The focus is on what will be happening then."}]},{"label":"Longer future background","color":"blue","description":"All day tomorrow, later tonight, or next week at this time help you see the future situation as a longer process.","examples":[{"en":"All day tomorrow, I will be studying for the exam.","ua":"The future day is presented as a longer process."},{"en":"Next week at this time, they will be staying in Prague.","ua":"We show a situation that will already be in progress."}]},{"label":"When / While","color":"amber","description":"When often adds another event or moment, and while shows parallel future background.","examples":[{"en":"I will be working when you call.","ua":"One action will be happening at the time of another event."},{"en":"While you are sleeping, we will be driving across Poland.","ua":"One situation becomes the background for another."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Typical combinations","intro":"See how time expressions connect with Future Continuous.","rows":[{"en":"This time tomorrow, I will be flying to Berlin.","ua":"this time tomorrow","note":"looking at the future from a future point"},{"en":"At 8 p.m. tomorrow, we will be watching the match.","ua":"at 8 p.m. tomorrow","note":"an exact future moment"},{"en":"Later tonight, she will be working on the report.","ua":"later tonight","note":"part of the future evening seen as a process"},{"en":"I will be waiting when you arrive.","ua":"when","note":"a process at the moment of another action"},{"en":"While you are resting, they will be setting up the room.","ua":"while","note":"parallel future background"}],"warning":"After when, we often use Present Simple or another natural form, not will: when you arrive, not when you will arrive."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Using Simple instead of a process at an exact future moment.","wrong":"❌ At 8 p.m. tomorrow, I work.","right":"✅ At 8 p.m. tomorrow, I will be working."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Using will after when.","wrong":"❌ I will be cooking when you will arrive.","right":"✅ I will be cooking when you arrive."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Forgetting -ing after will be with a time expression.","wrong":"❌ This time tomorrow, they will be travel to Kyiv.","right":"✅ This time tomorrow, they will be travelling to Kyiv."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["this time tomorrow, at 8 p.m. tomorrow, and tomorrow at ... often point to an action in progress in the future.","next week at this time, all day tomorrow, and later tonight work well for longer future background.","when often introduces another event or moment, and while shows parallel background.","After when, do not use will in a normal future time clause."]}
